reforms.description: The government is reforming the system of housing benefits with a new Housing Assistance Payment that will depend on income, helping to reduce work disincentives and improve employment incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing disincentives to work, the reform is expected to increase labor market participation and disposable income for low-income families.
reforms.status_evidence: The new Housing Assistance Payment is being rolled out to beneficiaries.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant issues in the housing benefits system, which can have a broad impact on labor market participation and income levels.
reforms.source_quote: In 2015, the government is also reforming the system of housing benefits with a new Housing Assistance Payment that will depend on income whether from welfare or work.

reforms.description: Ireland's government has committed to rebalancing innovation support towards direct grants, moving away from an over-reliance on R&D tax credits to enhance public support for business R&D, particularly benefiting young firms and SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Shifting to direct grants is expected to enhance support for R&D, particularly for young firms and SMEs, thereby fostering innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to innovation funding, indicating a major policy change.
reforms.source_quote: Public support for business R&D is increasingly skewed towards R&D tax credits. Rebalance innovation support towards direct grants.

reforms.description: Knowledge Transfer Ireland (KTI) was established to facilitate access to and use of ideas developed through publicly-funded research, aiming to enhance the commercialization of research outputs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to publicly-funded research ideas is expected to enhance commercialization, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: KTI has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While not a major reform, KTI represents a significant effort to improve knowledge transfer and commercialization.
reforms.source_quote: Knowledge Transfer Ireland (KTI), established in 2013, aims to make it easier for companies to access and use ideas developed through publicly-funded research.

reforms.description: The Irish Research Council continues to develop enterprise scholarship schemes to enhance collaboration between higher education institutions and industry, particularly targeting SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry is expected to improve the skills of the workforce and foster innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The enterprise scholarship schemes are currently in development and operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to strengthen ties between education and industry.
reforms.source_quote: The Irish Research Council continues to develop its two enterprise scholarship schemes: the Employment-Based Postgraduate and the Enterprise Partnership Scheme.

reforms.description: Ireland is reforming its apprenticeship system to expand beyond the construction sector and increase participation among women, with a new apprenticeship council established to evaluate proposals for development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding the apprenticeship system is expected to enhance workforce skills and participation, particularly among underrepresented groups.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the approach to apprenticeships in Ireland.
reforms.source_quote: A call for proposals to develop apprenticeships in areas beyond construction trades... was issued in January 2015.

reforms.description: The JobPath initiative is being rolled out to activate long-term unemployed individuals through private providers, with service provision expected to start in the latter half of 2015.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Activating long-term unemployed individuals is expected to improve labor market participation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to address long-term unemployment in Ireland.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are rolling out the JobPath initiative, whereby private providers will be in charge of activating the long-term unemployed.

reforms.description: The FIT initiative, involving leading high-tech companies, aims to bridge the gap between the ICT industry and educational institutions by promoting technology-based training programs that combine classroom learning with workplace experience.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Bridging the gap between industry and education is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce and promote innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The FIT initiative is operational and involves collaboration with high-tech companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to improve the alignment of education with industry needs.
reforms.source_quote: FIT is an industry-led initiative, involving leading high-tech companies such as IBM, and aims to act as a bridge between the ICT industry and colleges.

reforms.description: Google's sponsorship of a postgraduate certificate course at Trinity College Dublin aims to improve STEM education through innovative teaching practices and technology integration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving STEM education is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce and support innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The course is being offered at Trinity College Dublin with Google's sponsorship.
reforms.importance_rationale: This sponsorship represents a significant effort to enhance STEM education in Ireland.
reforms.source_quote: Google has provided funding of EUR 1.5 million to the College to support the programme.
